content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

July 13, 2004

Mark van de Kamp
City Manager
City of Santa Maria
110 East Cook Street, Room #1
Santa Maria, CA 93454-5190

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-04-130

Dear Mr. van de Kamp:

This letter is in response to your request on behalf of City of Santa Maria Mayor
Larry Lavagnino for advice regarding the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
"Act").¹

QUESTION

Is the City of Santa Maria (the "city") permitted under the Act to post on its
website a message welcoming visitors ("welcome message") to the city from Mayor
Lavagnino?

CONCLUSION

Because the Act's mass mailing prohibition does not apply to electronic mail or
websites, the city may post Mayor Lavagnino's welcome message on its website without
violating the prohibition. Furthermore, so long as the welcome message does not contain
express advocacy, does not make reference either to the mayor's candidacy for elective

___________________________
¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. A-04-130
Page No. 2

office or to that of his opponents, and does not solicit contributions, no contribution to the
mayor will result from the city's posting of the message.

FACTS

In your request for advice, you include the text of the proposed welcome message
from the mayor which the city intends to post on their website. As you note in your
letter, the message does not appear to contain any political statements, but instead, lists
the city's attractions and ends with an invitation to the reader to visit the city and explore
the website. The only reference to the mayor is his name at the end of the message
indicating that he is the author, and his city office address. Your concern about the
welcome message is prompted by the fact that the mayor will be campaigning for
reelection in the November 2004 election.

ANALYSIS

Your question implicates two aspects of the Act's requirements which we will
discuss below.

Is the Welcome Message a Prohibited Mass Mailing?

Section 89001 provides: "[n]o newsletter or other mass mailing shall be sent at
public expense." The purpose of this statute is to ensure that incumbents do not receive
an unfair advantage over challengers in the next election. (Section 81002(e).)
Regulation 18901 interprets this provision, in pertinent part, by providing:

"(a)(1) Any item sent is delivered, by any means, to the recipient at his or
her residence, place of employment or business, or post office box. For
purposes of this subdivision (a)(1), the item delivered to the recipient must
be a tangible item, such as a videotape, record, or button, or a written
document." [Emphasis added.]

According to regulation 18901, a publicly funded² mailing is a prohibited mass
mailing if, among other things, it is delivered as a tangible item to the recipient at his or
her residence, place of employment or business, or post office box. (Regulation
18901(a)(1).) Consistent with subdivision (a)(1), web pages are not considered tangible
items and therefore are not subject to either regulation 18901 or section 89001. (Smith
Advice Letter, No. A-03-177; Peterson Advice Letter, No. A-99-013; Foote Advice
Letter, No. A-98-114.)

_______________
² Although section 85300 restricts the use of public funds ("No public officer shall expend and no
candidate shall accept any public moneys for the purpose of seeking elective office"), regulation 18530
interprets section 85300 to prohibit only "the payment of public moneys, in the form of matching funds or
cash subsidies, for the public financing of elections."

File No. A-04-130
Page No. 3


Is the "Welcome Message" a Contribution from the City to the Mayor?

Section 82015 provides that an expenditure, a term defined in section 82025 as
any payment made for a political purpose, made at the behest³ of a candidate is a
contribution to the candidate unless full and adequate consideration is received
making the expenditure. A payment is "made for political purposes" when it is made
"[f]or the purpose of influencing or attempting to influence the action of the voters for or
against the nomination or election of a candidate . . . ." (Regulation 18225(a)(1).)
However, regulation 18215 excludes from the definition of "contribution" certain
communications made at the behest of a candidate. Specifically, regulation 18215
excludes from the definition of "contribution" a communication by the candidate or any
other person, if the communication:

"(i) Does not contain express advocacy;⁴

(ii) Does not make reference to the candidate's candidacy for elective office, the
candidate's election campaign, or the candidate's or his or her opponent's
qualifications for office; and

(iii) Does not solicit contributions to the candidate or to third persons for use in
support of the candidate or in opposition to the candidate's opponent."
(Regulation 18215(c)(4).) (Footnote added.)

Consequently, so long as the welcome message does not urge readers to either
vote for Mayor Lavagnino or against his opponent, does not refer to his election
campaign, and does not solicit contributions, the city's installation of the mayor's
welcome message on the city's website will not be considered a contribution to Mayor
Lavagnino.




³ "'Made at the behest of' means made under the control or at the direction of, in cooperation,
consultation, coordination, or concert with, at the request or suggestion of, or with the express, prior
consent of." (Regulation 18225.7(a).)

⁴ "A communication 'expressly advocates' the nomination, election or defeat of a candidate...'if it
contains express words of advocacy such as 'vote for,' 'elect,' 'support,' 'cast your ballot,' 'vote against,'
'defeat,' 'reject,' 'sign petitions for' or otherwise refers to a clearly identified candidate or measure so that
the communication, taken as a whole, unambiguously urges a particular result in an election." (Regulation
18225(b)(1)(D)(2).)

File No. A-04-130
Page No. 4

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: Adrianne Korchmoros
Political Reform Consultant
